project.name: cs-agent-bot
project.description: A clean and modular Golang microservice for AI-powered customer service automation using Clean Architecture principles, handling conversational AI interactions and message processing for automated customer support.

layers.name: Application Entry
layers.description: Application entry points and server bootstrap including HTTP server and migration CLI

layers.name: HTTP Delivery Layer
layers.description: HTTP handlers, middleware, routing, request/response processing, and DTOs for the REST API

layers.name: Business Logic Layer
layers.description: Use cases implementing core business rules for triggers, webhooks, dashboard operations, and AI classification

layers.name: Data Access Layer
layers.description: Repository implementations for PostgreSQL data operations using Squirrel query builder

layers.name: Domain Entities
layers.description: Core domain entity structs, filters, and type definitions representing the business domain

layers.name: Shared Packages
layers.description: Cross-cutting utilities including error handling, logging, pagination, database clients, and validation

layers.name: Database Schema
layers.description: SQL migration files defining and evolving the PostgreSQL database schema for multi-tenant workspace management

layers.name: Infrastructure & CI/CD
layers.description: Docker containerization, CI/CD pipelines, build configuration, and deployment automation

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation, setup guides, database schema docs, and API specifications

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Application configuration, environment variables, linter settings, and module dependencies

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand the project's purpose: a Golang microservice for AI-powered customer service automation. It covers setup, architecture, Docker deployment, and the Clean Architecture layering that organizes the entire codebase.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: The server main.go bootstraps the entire application — loading configuration, connecting to PostgreSQL and Redis, initializing OpenTelemetry tracing, wiring up all dependencies, and starting the HTTP server. This is the single point where all layers come together.
tour.languageLesson: Go's cmd/ directory convention places each binary in its own subdirectory. The internal/ directory prevents external packages from importing implementation details — enforced at the compiler level.

tour.title: Configuration & Dependencies
tour.description: The config package reads environment variables for database connections, Redis, API keys, and application settings. The deps package wires all repositories, usecases, and handlers together using constructor injection — the foundation of the Clean Architecture approach.

tour.title: HTTP Routing & Middleware
tour.description: The route.go file defines all API endpoints, mapping URL paths to handlers with middleware chains. The middleware layer handles cross-cutting concerns: workspace ID extraction from headers, JWT authentication, OIDC, HMAC verification, request logging, tracing, and error recovery.

tour.title: Dashboard API Handlers
tour.description: The dashboard handlers serve the management UI, providing CRUD operations for clients, invoices, escalations, templates, and activity logs. Each handler extracts the workspace ID from context and delegates to the usecase layer. The workspace_handler returns workspace metadata including holding workspace member relationships.

tour.title: Domain Entities
tour.description: The entity package defines the core domain model: Client, Invoice, Escalation, Workspace, Template, and ActivityLog. The Workspace entity introduces the holding workspace pattern — where IsHolding=true and MemberIDs enables aggregating data across multiple child workspaces.
tour.languageLesson: Go structs with JSON tags (e.g., `json:"company_id"`) control serialization. Filter structs like ClientFilter use []string for WorkspaceIDs to support querying across multiple workspaces for holding workspace expansion.

tour.title: Business Logic Layer
tour.description: The dashboard usecase orchestrates workspace-aware data fetching. When a holding workspace ID arrives, resolveWorkspaceIDs expands it to all member workspace IDs before querying. The trigger usecases automate customer outreach — renewal reminders, invoice follow-ups, health checks, and cross-sell campaigns.

tour.title: Data Access Repositories
tour.description: Repositories implement PostgreSQL data access using the Squirrel query builder. The client_repo demonstrates the workspace filtering pattern — buildClientFilter uses pq.Array with ANY() to support querying by multiple workspace IDs. Each repository follows the same interface/implementation pattern with context-based timeouts and tracing.
tour.languageLesson: Go interfaces are satisfied implicitly — any type implementing all methods of WorkspaceRepository automatically satisfies it, enabling clean dependency injection without explicit implements declarations.

tour.title: Database Schema & Migrations
tour.description: The migration directory contains numbered SQL files defining the complete database schema. Key tables include clients, invoices, escalations, workspaces, and workspace_users. The workspace migration introduces multi-tenancy with workspace_id foreign keys and the holding workspace pattern using UUID arrays.
tour.languageLesson: PostgreSQL UUID[] arrays store member workspace IDs directly in the workspaces table, enabling efficient holding workspace expansion without a separate join table.

tour.title: Shared Utilities
tour.description: Cross-cutting packages provide error handling (apperror with structured error codes), context utilities (workspace ID propagation), pagination helpers, database client initialization, and OpenTelemetry tracing setup. These are imported across all layers.

tour.title: Webhook & AI Integration
tour.description: The webhook handlers process incoming WhatsApp messages, classifying customer replies via the HaloAI client and routing them through the conversation state machine. The handoff handler manages transitions between bot and human support agents.

tour.title: Containerization & Deployment
tour.description: The Dockerfile builds an optimized Go binary in a multi-stage build. Docker Compose orchestrates the app with PostgreSQL and Redis for local development. GitHub Actions workflows handle staging and production deployments to Google Cloud Platform.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds for Go: the builder stage compiles a static binary with CGO_ENABLED=0, then the final stage copies just the binary into a minimal alpine image — typically under 20MB.
